lyrics:
CARD: Intro / sh\_l1 (00:00:00.000–00:00:31.909)
LYRIC: Seaward slack, a stay unset
SCENE: A 32 foot sloop named “Seaward” written in script font on the boat’s transom is making way at dawn. A young woman @Seaward Johnson is at the helm. Through memory flashbacks we see @Young Seaward Johnson then @Tad Johnson her father, then @Middle-aged Seaward Johnson, then @Mature Seaward Johnson setting the stage for what’s to come
PROMPT: A lyrical passage through memory and tide. Dynamic shifts in intensity guide scene pacing, balancing quiet reflection with rising resolve. The shoreline frames a journey from drift to acceptance
ACTION: Transition 00:00:36.175
CARD: sh\_l1 (00:00:31.910–00:00:36.175)
LYRIC: Seaward slack, a stay unset
SCENE: A frustrated @Seaward Johnson at the helm of her sailboat stares off in the distance
PROMPT: A frustrated @Seaward Johnson at the helm of her sailboat stares off in the distance
ACTION: Transition 00:00:36.175
CARD: sh\_l2 (00:00:36.185–00:00:40.403)
LYRIC: She hides the things she can’t forget
SCENE: @Seaward Johnson sees the face of @Ramona Perez a former lover in a bed room
PROMPT: @Seaward Johnson sees the face of @Ramona Perez a former lover in a bed room
ACTION: Transition 00:00:40.403
CARD: sh\_l3 (00:00:56.430–00:01:04.586)
LYRIC: A windward silhouette
SCENE: @Ramona Perez in the shower behind thin curtain
PROMPT: @Seaward Johnson’s bedroom from the bed into the bathroom
ACTION: Transition 00:01:04.586
CARD: sh\_l4 (00:01:21.299–00:01:29.002)
LYRIC: Something waiting
SCENE: @Seaward Johnson alone in a bar waiting
PROMPT: An empty bar at midnight
ACTION: Transition 00:01:29.002
CARD: sh\_l5 (00:01:31.591–00:01:36.253)
LYRIC: She’s drawing, a trailing sea
SCENE: @Radon Topaz a very attractive black man enters the bar and @Seaward Johnson watches him approach with desire
PROMPT: An empty bar at midnight
ACTION: Transition 00:01:36.253
CARD: sh\_l6 (00:01:36.263–00:01:40.867)
LYRIC: A pulsing urge to feel the lee
SCENE: @Radon Topaz and @Seaward Johnson sitting too close
PROMPT: An empty bar at midnight
ACTION: Transition 00:01:40.867
CARD: sh\_l7 (00:01:43.224–00:01:48.667)
LYRIC: A drifting story
SCENE: @Ramona Perez finds @Radon Topaz and @Seaward Johnson in bed together
PROMPT: @Seaward Johnson’s bedroom
ACTION: Transition 00:01:48.667
CARD: sh\_l8 (00:01:48.722–00:01:54.091)
LYRIC: Something fading
SCENE: @Seaward Johnson on her sloop leaning over the railing looking at her reflection in the sea
PROMPT: Calm seas mid-day light
ACTION: Transition 00:01:54.091
CARD: sh\_l9 (00:01:55.525–00:02:00.698)
LYRIC: Seaward sings her soft refrain
SCENE: @Seaward Johnson watching the seagulls fly overhead from her sloop
PROMPT: The soft refrain is the time, like water or wind, rushing by
ACTION: Transition 00:02:00.698
CARD: sh\_l10 (00:02:00.848–00:02:05.916)
LYRIC: Some things are lost and some remain
SCENE: The sloop is leaving the harbor
PROMPT: The sloop is leaving the harbor
ACTION: Transition 00:02:05.916
CARD: sh\_l11 (00:02:07.269–00:02:12.277)
LYRIC: The telltales caution “mind the vane”
SCENE: On the sloop the telltale shimmers as the main sail takes wind
PROMPT: On the sloop the telltale shimmers as the main sail takes wind
ACTION: Transition 00:02:12.277
CARD: sh\_l12 (00:02:12.287–00:02:17.236)
LYRIC: A rising tide when Seaward calls
SCENE: The sloop makes way as another memory displays @Young Seaward Johnson
PROMPT: @Young Seaward Johnson standing on a dock watching the sloop piloted by @Tad Johnson motor from the harbor
ACTION: Transition 00:02:17.236
CARD: sh\_l10a - B-roll Transition (Bar → Office, 00:02:20)
Scene: Close-up of water pouring into a sink dissolving into open sea
Prompt: Match cut symbolizing time’s flow between desire and ambition. Maintain color continuity: amber to cool white
CARD: sh\_l13 (00:02:31.777–00:02:36.654)
LYRIC: Mast firm set, her headsail clean
SCENE: @Middle-aged Seaward Johnson in business attire at a desk interviewing for a job
PROMPT: Business conference room with an interviewer
ACTION: Transition 00:02:36.654
CARD: sh\_l14 (00:02:36.664–00:02:41.483)
LYRIC: She sets her track to things unseen
SCENE: @Middle-aged Seaward Johnson working at her work desk
PROMPT: @Middle-aged Seaward Johnson working at her work desk
ACTION: Transition 00:02:41.483
CARD: sh\_l15 (00:02:42.772–00:02:47.258)
LYRIC: A wake without a sounding trace
SCENE: @Middle-aged Seaward Johnson presenting to a group of people
PROMPT: @Middle-aged Seaward Johnson looks slightly uncomfortable as she presents
ACTION: Transition 00:02:47.258
CARD: sh\_l16 (00:02:47.268–00:02:51.754)
LYRIC: Something silent missed
SCENE: @Middle-aged Seaward finishes her presentation in the conference room
PROMPT: As she finishes she looks out the window and remembers the call of the sea
ACTION: Transition 00:02:51.754
CARD: sh\_l17 (00:02:51.764–00:02:56.250)
LYRIC: Her compass cast in pale reflection
SCENE: @Middle-aged Seaward still looking out the window of the conference room sees @Ramona Perez and @Radon Topaz in reflections of longing
PROMPT: @Middle-aged Seaward still looking out the window of the conference room sees @Ramona Perez and @Radon Topaz in reflections of longing
ACTION: Transition 00:02:56.250
CARD: sh\_l18 (00:02:56.260–00:03:00.747)
LYRIC: Still she draws her destination
SCENE: @Middle-aged Seaward moves toward her office
PROMPT: @Middle-aged Seaward looks slightly sad
ACTION: Transition 00:03:00.747
CARD: sh\_l19 (00:03:00.814–00:03:05.300)
LYRIC: A quiet contradiction
SCENE: @Middle-aged Seaward in a new bigger office
PROMPT: @Middle-aged Seaward sitting in her big new office. A picture of her sloop and her father @Tad Johnson sits on her desk next to a name plate which says “Seaward Johnson - Director”
ACTION: Transition 00:03:05.300
CARD: sh\_l20 (00:03:05.310–00:03:09.796)
LYRIC: Something dismissed
SCENE: @Middle-aged Seaward in her big new office
PROMPT: @Middle-aged Seaward picks up the picture of the sloop and smiles
ACTION: Transition 00:03:09.796
Card: sh\_l20a - B-roll Transition (Office → Retirement, 00:03:12)
Scene: Pendulum clock in office swings; crossfade to boat boom swaying in same rhythm
Prompt: Passage of time and rhythm of labor. Maintain color continuity: cool to warm light
CARD: sh\_l21 (00:03:09.881–00:03:14.367)
LYRIC: Seaward sings her soft refrain
SCENE: @Middle-aged Seaward watching the seagulls fly overhead from her sloop
PROMPT: The soft refrain is the time, like water or wind, rushing by
ACTION: Transition 00:03:14.367
CARD: sh\_l22 (00:03:14.456–00:03:18.942)
LYRIC: Some things are lost and some remain
SCENE: @Middle-aged Seaward on the sloop anchored just off an island
PROMPT: The sloop is safe and secure in the harbor
ACTION: Transition 00:03:18.942
CARD: sh\_l23 (00:03:18.952–00:03:23.439)
LYRIC: The telltales bold “you found your name”
SCENE: @Middle-aged Seaward in a very professional suit in a business setting
PROMPT: @Middle-aged Seaward celebrating with business members her big promotion
ACTION: Transition 00:03:23.439
CARD: sh\_l24 (00:03:23.449–00:03:27.935)
LYRIC: A flooding tide as Seaward calls
SCENE: @Middle-aged Seaward at an expensive car dealership
PROMPT: @Middle-aged Seaward has bought an expensive new car to celebrate her promotion
ACTION: Transition 00:03:27.935
CARD: sh\_l25 (00:03:28.045–00:03:32.531)
LYRIC: She bends the sheet, begins to heel
SCENE: We meet @Mature Seaward Johnson
PROMPT: @Mature Seaward Johnson in her big office years later. Things packed and a box in her hand. She is retiring
ACTION: Transition 00:03:32.531
CARD: sh\_l26 (00:03:32.724–00:03:37.210)
LYRIC: Remembers how the stays feel
SCENE: A rush of memories
PROMPT: A rush of memories - @Ramona Perez, @Radon Topaz, @Young Seaward, @Tad Johnson and the sloop
ACTION: Transition 00:03:37.210
CARD: sh\_l27 (00:03:37.220–00:03:41.698)
LYRIC: Too few hours left to steal
SCENE: @Mature Seaward Johnson’s home
PROMPT: @Mature Seaward Johnson alone in a comfortable home, looking lonely
ACTION: Transition 00:03:41.698
CARD: sh\_l28 (00:03:44.636–00:03:50.410)
LYRIC: A ship upon a reef
SCENE: @Mature Seaward Johnson sitting in a recliner in her home
PROMPT: @Mature Seaward Johnson resting in an easy chair
ACTION: Transition 00:03:50.410
CARD: sh\_l29 (00:03:50.420–00:03:56.188)
LYRIC: She dreams the drawing mainsail
SCENE: A vision of @Seaward on the sloop moving fast
PROMPT: @Seaward at the helm in a gale with the sail luffing
ACTION: Transition 00:03:56.188
CARD: sh\_l30 (00:03:59.131–00:04:05.016)
LYRIC: The breakers do prevail
SCENE: @Mature Seaward Johnson in a hospital in a wheelchair wearing a leg brace
PROMPT: @Mature Seaward Johnson is wheelchaired to a waiting car after surgery on her knee
ACTION: Transition 00:04:05.016
CARD: sh\_l31 (00:04:05.026–00:04:10.910)
LYRIC: No longer subject to the gale
SCENE: @Mature Seaward Johnson in a physical therapy office rehabbing her knee
PROMPT: @Mature Seaward Johnson trying to regain her mobility
ACTION: Transition 00:04:10.910
CARD: sh\_l32 (00:04:10.920–00:04:16.795)
LYRIC: Time is not the thief
SCENE: @Mature Seaward Johnson walking slowly in her beautiful backyard
PROMPT: @Mature Seaward Johnson is slowly walking through her garden. A sense of grace and acceptance shows on her face
ACTION: Transition 00:04:16.795
CARD: sh\_l33 (00:04:17.637–00:04:22.360)
LYRIC: Seaward sings her soft refrain
SCENE: @Mature Seaward Johnson’s face in close-up
PROMPT: @Mature Seaward Johnson has gained acceptance with time. It is neither thief nor evil. It just is
ACTION: Transition 00:04:22.360
CARD: sh\_l30a - B-roll Transition (Reflection → Outro, 00:04:45)
Scene: Sunlight gliding across desk, gradually turning into shimmering tide
Prompt: Time dissolves into the sea; prepare entry to metaphoric outro. Maintain color continuity: warm to silver-white
CARD: sh\_l34 (00:04:22.370–00:04:27.094)
LYRIC: Some things are lost and some remain
SCENE: Montage of memories of her life and the people in it
PROMPT: Montage of memories of her life and the people in it from previous scenes
ACTION: Transition 00:04:27.094
CARD: sh\_l35 (00:04:27.250–00:04:31.973)
LYRIC: The telltales sigh “not the same”
SCENE: @Mature Seaward Johnson looking at the trees in her backyard
PROMPT: @Mature Seaward Johnson looks up at the trees in her yard as they gently sway in a soft breeze
ACTION: Transition 00:04:31.973
CARD: sh\_l36 (00:04:31.983–00:04:36.706)
LYRIC: A falling tide as Seaward calls
SCENE: @Mature Seaward Johnson’s backyard
PROMPT: @Mature Seaward Johnson sits and closes her eyes as a sweet smile crosses her face
ACTION: Transition 00:04:36.706

CARD: sh\_l37 - The Call (00:05:14–00:05:34)
Scene: Mature Seaward’s garden; wind rises, light flickers across her face
Prompt: 'The Call' - leaves and waves intercut. Maintain color continuity: golden to silver
Mood: Awakening, invitation. Action: Transition 00:05:34

CARD: sh\_l38 - The Return of the Tide (00:05:34–00:05:54)
Scene: Camera tilts skyward; clouds drift, dissolve into waves
Prompt: 'The Return of the Tide' - wind becomes tide, color shift green→blue-silver
Mood: Gathering power. Action: Transition 00:05:54

CARD: sh\_l39 - Memory’s Wake (00:05:54–00:06:10)
Scene: Overlapping transparencies - Young, Middle-aged, and Mature Seaward moving as one
Prompt: 'Memory’s Wake' - layered dissolve of lives in rhythmic motion
Mood: Crescendo, union. Action: Transition 00:06:10

CARD: sh\_l40 - Dissolution (00:06:10–00:06:24)
Scene: Composite image dissolves into pure light and spray; sloop becomes luminous ink
Prompt: 'Dissolution' - upward float through particles of light
Mood: Ecstatic surrender. Action: Transition 00:06:24

CARD: sh\_l41 - The Breath (00:06:24–00:06:36)
Scene: Wide aerial over calm ocean at dawn; horizon glows
Prompt: 'The Breath' - wind and distant gulls remain; reflection faint in sky
Mood: Tranquil exhale. Action: Transition 00:06:36

CARD: sh\_l42 - Silence / Return (00:06:36–00:06:44)
Scene: Static frame of horizon dissolving to white, then black
Prompt: 'Silence / Return' - final wind becomes breath; optional text: 'Some things are lost, and some remain.'
Mood: Stillness, completion. Action: Fade out 00:06:44
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track unfolds with a spacious and introspective texture, creating a searching, slightly melancholic atmosphere through clean, reverberant guitars and a gentle vocal delivery. The feel is moderately energetic and propulsive, driven by a steady rhythm section that provides a foundation without overwhelming the delicate melodic elements. The production is exceptionally clean and wide, giving each instrument its own distinct space to breathe, resulting in a mix that feels both intimate and expansive. The song's most distinctive quality is its powerful dynamic arc: it builds from restrained, narrative verses into soaring, anthemic choruses where layered vocals and swelling guitars create moments of immense emotional release.

review.review_text: PCBender has built something genuinely ambitious here: a six-and-a-half minute song in C major that tracks a woman's life through sailing metaphors, and it earns every second of its runtime. The verse writing is precise in the way good nautical language always is, with lines like 'the telltales caution mind the vane' doing double duty as rigging instruction and quiet life advice, and the chorus refrain 'some things are lost and some remain' lands with the plainspoken weight of a John Hiatt lyric. The production does exactly what the song needs, holding back through the searching, conversational verses before the guitars and layered vocals open up into something close to anthemic, the kind of lift that makes you want to replay the chorus just to live in it again. This is PCBender at his most purposeful.
anchors_used: lyric: 'the telltales caution mind the vane'
lyric: 'some things are lost and some remain'
dynamic arc from verses to choruses
